title: Impeaching Paul Adam Engelmayer, United States District Court Judge for the Southern District of New York, for high crimes and misdemeanors.
plain_summary: This bill is about removing a federal judge named Paul Adam Engelmayer from his job. The House of Representatives says the judge did wrong things by using his power as a judge for his own personal reasons and political beliefs. They claim he stopped President Trump and Treasury Secretary Bessent from getting important government records they needed from the Treasury Department. The House thinks this was so wrong that the judge should be impeached, which means removed from being a judge.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This is a resolution to impeach Paul Adam Engelmayer. He is a United States District Court Judge for the Southern District of New York. The House of Representatives wants to remove him from his job for high crimes and misdemeanors (serious wrongdoing while in office).

sections.title: Resolution Statement
sections.text: The House of Representatives has decided that Judge Paul Adam Engelmayer should be impeached for high crimes and misdemeanors. They are sending this article of impeachment to the United States Senate. This article explains the charges against the judge and represents both the House and the American people.

sections.title: Article I: Abuse of Power
sections.text: Judge Paul Engelmayer is accused of breaking his oath of office. The charges say he used his position as a judge to help his own personal interests and political goals instead of being fair and impartial. The Constitution says the House of Representatives has the sole power to impeach federal officers, including judges. It also says the President has broad authority to control the executive branch and its departments. Judge Engelmayer violated his oath to the Constitution and his duty to be impartial to the American people. He abused his judicial powers by putting his personal and political connections above his duty to be fair to the public and people in court cases. The specific charges are that Judge Engelmayer stopped President Trump and Secretary Bessent from giving access to Treasury Department records, payment systems, and data systems that contain personal information and confidential financial information. He also stopped them from giving access to political appointees, special government employees, and government workers from other agencies to Treasury Department payment records and systems with personal information. This behavior shows apparent bias and favoritism. It represents an abuse of judicial power and hurts how the court system works. Judge Engelmayer used his high office to interfere with what the people wanted. He used his position to take actions that went beyond his proper judicial authority. By making a political decision outside his legal duties, he damaged the impartiality of our court system. Therefore, Judge Engelmayer is guilty of high crimes and misdemeanors and should be removed from office.
